codeReview.summary: The implementation comprehensively hardens webhook claim/idempotency semantics and cron recovery endpoints. It adds 4 new test files and enhances existing tests to cover all targeted validation assertions (VAL-API-006 through VAL-API-010, VAL-CROSS-004, and VAL-CROSS-008). The worker correctly followed TDD principles, added failing tests first, then implemented minimal changes to make them pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the established pattern for exposing internal functions for testability by exporting runDispatchAckTimeoutCron from route.ts.
All new tests use the standardized lease owner prefix pattern (e.g., 'cron:dispatch-ack-timeout', 'cron:v3') for preventing duplicate processing across parallel workers.

The worker correctly followed the delivery-loop-runtime-worker skill procedure: identified impacted runtime paths, added failing characterization tests first, implemented minimum changes, and ran targeted validators.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows: 1) dispatch-ack-timeout tests (4 tests) for VAL-API-009/010, 2) scheduled-tasks tests (9 tests) for VAL-API-008/VAL-CROSS-004/008, 3) webhook tests (6 tests) for VAL-API-006/007

summary: Feature m3-harden-webhook-cron-recovery successfully implements comprehensive test coverage for webhook claim/idempotency semantics and cron recovery endpoints. All 19 new tests pass, covering VAL-API-006 through VAL-API-010, VAL-CROSS-004, and VAL-CROSS-008. The implementation properly hardens the delivery-loop ingress layer against duplicate processing and ensures deterministic watchdog behavior.
